query: current status of artificial blood cell technology including FDA approval and clinical use

retrieved_docs.title: Chimeric Antigen Receptor T-Cell Therapy -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: For administering both CAR-T cell treatments, it is important to confirm the patient’s identity with the patient identifiers on the infusion bag, as they are all for autologous use. A leuodepleting filter cannot be used, and central venous access is the recommended method for infusion. Interleukin-6 antagonists (ie, tocilizumab or siltuximab), corticosteroids, and emergent equipment are needed before infusion and during the recovery period in case of side effects.

retrieved_docs.title: Luspatercept -- Indications -- FDA-Approved Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Luspatercept is also indicated for treating anemia in adults with very low-to-intermediate risk myelodysplastic syndromes (MDS) who are ESA-naïve and require regular red blood cell (RBC) transfusions. Luspatercept is not a substitute for red blood cell transfusions in patients requiring rapid anemia correction.

retrieved_docs.title: Luspatercept -- Indications -- FDA-Approved Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Luspatercept is an erythroid maturation agent approved by the FDA for treating anemia in patients with β-thalassemia who need regular red blood cell transfusions. [1] The recommended dose of luspatercept for patients with β-thalassemia is 1 mg/kg subcutaneously once every 3 weeks. [2] [3] The FDA approved this dosage based on the results of the phase III BELIEVE trial. [4] This medication promotes late-stage erythropoiesis, even in patients with β-thalassemia ineffectively controlled with erythropoiesis-stimulating agents (ESA).

retrieved_docs.title: Filgrastim -- Indications -- FDA Indications
retrieved_docs.content: To mobilize hematopoietic progenitor cells to the peripheral blood for collection by leukapheresis in patients undergoing peripheral blood progenitor cell collection and therapy. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplantation in Sickle Cell Disease -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: In September 2018, the National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute (NHLBI) launched the Cure Sickle Cell Initiative. Under this initiative, efforts are being made to develop new genetic approaches to cure SCD. [4] [5] Advancement in gene therapy techniques has shown promising results in preclinical and clinical trials, but formal approval by the United States Food and Drug Administration (FDA) is still awaited. [2] [3] [6] [7]

retrieved_docs.title: CART Cell Therapy Toxicity -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Laboratory evaluation of ICANS includes biomarkers similar to CRS in addition to a lumbar puncture to analyze cerebrospinal fluid, neuroimaging, and EEG to determine the extent of damage from ICANS and rule out other organic factors and/or sinister causes, including but not limited to infections. [41]

retrieved_docs.title: Biosimilars Use in Medicine for Inflammatory Diseases -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Used as solo therapy for low-grade or follicular CD20-positive B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma in adult subjects that have relapsed or refractory to treatment.

retrieved_docs.title: Genetics, Histone Code -- Clinical Significance -- Current FDA Approved Drugs
retrieved_docs.content: Histone deacetylation leads to the repression of a specific gene. Drugs that inhibit deacetylation, known as histone deacetylase inhibitors (HDACi’s), lead to decreased histone deacetylation and ultimately increased gene expression. There are four HDACi’s currently approved by the FDA. Vorinostat obtained approval in 2006 for the treatment of cutaneous t-cell lymphoma. [9] Romidepsin is a natural HDACi isolated from bacterial fermentation of Chromobacterium violaceum and was approved in 2009 for the treatment of cutaneous t-cell lymphoma.  Belinostat received approval in 2014, and panobinostat approval came in 2015, both under the FDA’s accelerated approval program, for the treatment of relapsed peripheral t-cell lymphoma and relapsed multiple myeloma, respectively. [10] Many other HDACi’s are currently under investigation. [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Chimeric Antigen Receptor T-Cell Therapy -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: Both medications have no contraindications per packing insert, but caution is advised in patients with autoimmune disorders and solid organ transplants.

retrieved_docs.title: Chimeric Antigen Receptor T-Cell Therapy -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: The first CAR-T cell treatment approved by the United States Food and Drug Administration(FDA) in August 2017 was tisagenlecleucel. It is indicated in patients up to 25 years of age with B-cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia that is refractory or in the second or later relapse. It also has indications in adult patients with relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphoma after 2 or more lines of systemic therapy, including diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) not otherwise specified, high-grade B-cell lymphoma and DLBCL arising from follicular lymphoma. The second US FDA-approved CAR-T cell treatment, in October 2017, is axicabtagene ciloleucel, which is indicated for the treatment of adult patients with relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphoma after 2 or more lines of systemic therapy, including DLBCL not otherwise specified, primary mediastinal large B-cell lymphoma, high-grade B-cell lymphoma, and DLBCL arising from follicular lymphoma. Neither treatment indicates patients with primary central nervous system(CNS) lymphoma. According to the US National Institute of Health clinical trial registration, multiple clinical trials are underway with CAR-T cell treatment for different malignancies, including multiple myeloma, CNS tumors, hepatocellular carcinoma, lung cancer, etc. [3] [4] [5] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplantation -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: Special equipment exists for collecting, preserving, and administering stem cell products.

retrieved_docs.title: Expiration Dating and National Drug Code Rules -- Function -- National Drug Code – Subpart A - General
retrieved_docs.content: Establishments that engage in the manufacturing, repackaging, relabeling, or salvaging human drugs regulated under the biologic license applications. These requirements apply to them unless they are required to register and list such drugs as human blood or blood product. Human whole blood and blood products do not include the plasma derivatives such as albumin, Immune Globulin, Factor VIII and Factor IX, and recombinant versions of plasma derivatives or animal-derived plasma derivatives, or bulk substance such as fractionation intermediates or pastes.

retrieved_docs.title: Pharmaceutics -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Initiating early clinical trials after establishing proof of a concept commence after an Investigational New Drug Application (IND) has been reviewed and approved by the FDA. Similar procedures are used in the EMA, Japan, and other countries, although some nations limit new drug testing for the first time. The initial studies in humans can be in actual patients if the drug's toxicity or target would not be appropriate for volunteers. Increasing doses of the drug are used to establish the drugs' safety and determine the drugs' pharmacokinetics (the duration and extent of the drug in the body) as well as to measure the drugs' effect on biomarkers (serum or tissue enzymes, histology, or any other measure of drug effect such as glucose in diabetes). This data will be used to select doses to be brought into a proof of concept or phase 2 studies in a select patient population. Clinical trials like the TeGenero disaster are fortunately the exception and not the rule. In this study, the first volunteers received a 500 times lower dose than the safe dose as established in animal studies. However, all six human volunteers faced life-threatening conditions involving multiorgan failure, which required transfer to the intensive care unit. As a result, the implementation of new precautions and toxicity calculations became standard since that 2006 incident. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Bortezomib -- Indications -- Non-FDA-Approved Uses
retrieved_docs.content: Treatment of antibody-mediated rejection in cardiac transplantation Relapsed or refractory cutaneous T-cell lymphomas Relapsed or refractory follicular lymphoma Relapsed or refractory peripheral T-cell lymphoma Systemic light-chain amyloidosis Relapsed or refractory Waldenstrom macroglobulinemia

retrieved_docs.title: CART Cell Therapy Toxicity -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Since then, more agents such as lisocabtagene maraleucel (liso-cel) have made their way onto the market, demonstrating better tolerance. [11] Later, brexucabtagene autoleucel was approved for R/R mantle cell lymphoma (MCL). [12] More recently, anti-B-cell Maturation Antigen (BCMA) CAR-T, idecabtagene vicleucel (ide-cel, KarMMa), and ciltacabtagene autoleucel (cilta-cel, CARTITUDE-1) have been approved for R/R multiple myeloma. [3] [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Lung Transplantation -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: EVLP has emerged as a promising solution to address the scarcity of donor organs. EVLP facilitates the evaluation and potential improvement of marginal donor lungs and mitigates risks associated with prolonged ischemic times due to logistical constraints. EVLP systems approved by the United States Food and Drug Administration (FDA) have shown favorable outcomes in clinical trials. Retrospective studies indicate that posttransplant survival rates for recipients of marginal donor lungs perfused using EVLP are comparable to those for recipients of standard criteria lungs preserved conventionally. Despite these promising results, the widespread adoption of EVLP has plateaued in recent years, primarily due to the substantial costs associated with establishing EVLP programs.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1396
retrieved_docs.content: O‑raffinose cross‑linked human hemoglobin and dextran are both classified as members of the group “blood substitutes and plasma protein fractions,” indicating that they serve as artificial blood products or plasma protein derivatives used to replace or augment the functions of red blood cells or plasma proteins in clinical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2820
retrieved_docs.content: INJECTIONS AND IMPLANTATIONS are classified as subprocedures within the broader categories of فصادة الكريات البيض (white‑blood‑cell apheresis) and نقل الدم الذاتي (autologous blood transfusion). Both parent procedures involve the manipulation, collection, or reinfusion of blood components, and the inclusion of INJECTIONS AND IMPLANTATIONS under these headings underscores their role in delivering therapeutic agents or devices directly into the patient’s circulatory system or tissues as part of blood‑related therapeutic strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1761
retrieved_docs.content: INJECTIONS AND IMPLANTATIONS is classified as a type of تسريب, which itself is a kind of نقل الدم (blood transfusion) and belongs to the broader category of الإجراءات العلاجية، غير مصنف في موضع آخر (therapeutic procedures not classified elsewhere). In addition to its relationship with تسريب, INJECTIONS AND IMPLANTATIONS is also linked to فصادة الكريات البيض (white‑blood‑cell apheresis) and to نقل الدم الذاتي (autologous blood transfusion), indicating that it shares characteristics with both apheresis procedures and self‑donated blood transfusion. نقل الدم encompasses نقل الدم الذاتي as a specific child category, and the term blood transfusion is used to classify نقل الدم. Blood transfusion is associated with complications of surgical procedures or medical care, highlighting that transfusion practices can be a cause of such complications. Together, these relationships illustrate how various procedural categories—INJECTIONS AND IMPLANTATIONS, تسريب, فصادة الكريات البيض, and نقل الدم الذاتي—are interconnected within the overarching framework of blood‑related therapeutic interventions and their potential impact on patient safety.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 2246
retrieved_docs.content: Tromethamin is categorized within the therapeutic class of Blood Products/Modifiers/Volume Expanders, indicating that it functions as a volume‑expanding agent used to restore or maintain intravascular fluid volume. This classification reflects its role in clinical situations such as hemorrhage, hypovolemia, or other conditions requiring rapid hemodynamic support, where augmenting plasma volume is essential. Recognizing tromethamin as part of the Blood Products/Modifiers/Volume Expanders class helps clinicians select appropriate volume‑expanding therapies and understand its mechanism of modifying blood components to increase circulatory volume.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 1477
retrieved_docs.content: INJECTIONS AND IMPLANTATIONS is positioned within both فصادة الكريات البيض (leukapheresis) and نقل الدم الذاتي (autologous blood transfusion), indicating that injection and implantation techniques are considered relevant to white‑blood‑cell filtration procedures as well as to the practice of using a patient’s own blood for transfusion. نقل الدم (blood transfusion) is classified as “blood transfusion” and serves as the parent category for نقل الدم الذاتي, establishing autologous blood transfusion as a specific subtype of the broader transfusion process. Physical Treatment Methods are also linked as a child of نقل الدم, suggesting that certain physical therapeutic approaches are conceptually grouped under the umbrella of blood transfusion practices. Importantly, نقل الدم is recognized as a potential cause of complications of surgical procedures or medical care, underscoring the need for careful risk assessment and management when transfusion is employed. Together, these relationships illustrate a hierarchical network in which injection/implantation procedures, leukapheresis, autologous transfusion, and related physical treatments are interconnected within the larger context of blood transfusion and its clinical implications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1522
retrieved_docs.content: Betibeglogene autotemcel is classified as a biologické přípravky, meaning it is a biological product (biologic). This categorization places betibeglogene autotemcel among therapeutics derived from biological sources, which influences its development, regulatory considerations, and clinical application.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 2 -- Community 485
retrieved_docs.content: Blood Products/Modifiers/Volume Expanders [TC] is a therapeutic category that encompasses a variety of agents, including the small‑molecule buffer citric acid (kyselina citronová), the coagulation‑supporting vitamin vitamin K 1, the buffering compound tromethamine, and the advanced gene‑therapy product betibeglogene autotemcel. Citric acid and tromethamine are commonly used to adjust pH and maintain stability in intravenous solutions, while vitamin K 1 is administered to correct or prevent deficiencies that affect blood clotting. Betibeglogene autotemcel, although primarily known as a gene‑modified autologous hematopoietic stem‑cell therapy for sickle cell disease, is also classified within this therapeutic class, reflecting its inclusion under the broader umbrella of biologic preparations. The mapping of betibeglogene autotemcel to biologické přípravky (biological products) further emphasizes its origin as a biologic agent. Together, these diverse substances illustrate the wide range of compounds—ranging from simple buffers and vitamins to complex biologics—grouped under the volume‑expander therapeutic class, highlighting the need for clinicians to recognize their distinct pharmacologic properties and appropriate clinical applications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 2 -- Community 421
retrieved_docs.content: Blood transfusion, known in Arabic as نقل الدم, is a medical procedure used to replace lost blood components and is classified under the broader concept of blood replacement therapy. It encompasses specific subtypes, including نقل الدم الذاتي (autologous blood transfusion), which involves collecting and reinfusing the patient’s own blood. Although blood transfusion is a valuable therapeutic intervention, it can also be a cause of complications related to surgical procedures or other medical care, underscoring the importance of careful indication, thorough screening, and vigilant monitoring to minimize adverse outcomes. Recognizing the hierarchical relationship between general blood transfusion, its child category autologous transfusion, and the potential for procedural complications is essential for safe and effective patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 3 -- Community 2237
retrieved_docs.content: Blood substitutes and perfusion solutions constitute a specific category of intravenous (IV) solutions that are administered parenterally to deliver fluids, electrolytes, and nutrients. As a subclass of IV solutions used for parenteral administration, these products are formulated to replace or augment blood components, provide rapid volume expansion, and supply essential nutrients when oral intake is not possible. They play a critical role in clinical settings such as fluid resuscitation for hypovolemia, management of blood loss, and provision of nutritional support for patients who require intravenous therapy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 766
retrieved_docs.content: BLOOD SUBSTITUTES AND PERFUSION SOLUTIONS are classified within the broader category of blood and related products, indicating that they belong to this group of medical substances used for transfusion, volume expansion, and related therapeutic purposes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 2 -- Community 698
retrieved_docs.content: Blood substitutes and plasma protein fractions comprise several distinct agents, including O‑raffinose cross‑linked human hemoglobin, dextran, and hydroxyethyl starch. These products are employed as volume expanders or oxygen‑carrying solutions in clinical settings where conventional blood transfusion is unavailable or unsuitable. While each agent belongs to the same therapeutic category, hydroxyethyl starch carries a specific safety limitation: it is contraindicated in patients with thrombotic disease (الاعتلال الخثري). Recognizing this contraindication is essential for clinicians to avoid exacerbating clot‑related conditions when selecting a blood substitute or plasma protein fraction for patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 3 -- Community 2058
retrieved_docs.content: Human factor VIII is used as a therapeutic agent to treat coagulation and hemorrhagic disorders, helping to correct deficient clotting activity and control bleeding. By supplementing the missing or insufficient factor VIII, it restores normal hemostasis in conditions such as hemophilia A and other bleeding disorders, thereby reducing the risk of severe hemorrhage and improving patient outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 3 -- Community 2249
retrieved_docs.content: INJECTIONS AND IMPLANTATIONS is categorized under تسريب, which itself is placed within the broader group of الإجراءات العلاجية، غير مصنف في موضع آخر (therapeutic procedures not classified elsewhere). تسريب is defined as a type of نقل الدم (blood transfusion), linking INJECTIONS AND IMPLANTATIONS to the blood transfusion domain. In addition to its relationship with تسريب, INJECTIONS AND IMPLANTATIONS is also associated with فصادة الكريات البيض (leukapheresis) and with نقل الدم الذاتي (autologous blood transfusion), reflecting its relevance to multiple specialized transfusion and apheresis procedures. نقل الدم serves as a parent category for نقل الدم الذاتي, indicating that self‑blood transfusion is a specific sub‑type of general blood transfusion. Physical Treatment Methods is likewise positioned under نقل الدم, suggesting that certain physical therapeutic techniques are considered part of the transfusion‑related treatment spectrum. The concept of blood transfusion is used to classify نقل الدم, reinforcing the hierarchical relationship between the general practice of transfusion and its specific categories. Finally, blood transfusion is recognized as a potential source of complications of surgical procedures or medical care, highlighting the need for careful management of transfusion‑related risks in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 447
retrieved_docs.content: Blood and related products constitute a broad category of therapeutic materials derived from or used in place of blood components. Within this category, the subclass Blood substitutes and plasma protein fractions is recognized as a specific type of blood and related product. The group Blood substitutes and plasma protein fractions includes individual agents such as O‑raffinose cross‑linked human hemoglobin and hydroxyethylový škrob, each representing a distinct formulation designed to mimic or support the functions of native blood. Moreover, the higher‑level classification BLOOD SUBSTITUTES AND PERFUSION SOLUTIONS is positioned as an overarching group that encompasses blood and related products, indicating that all items in the blood and related products hierarchy, including the Blood substitutes and plasma protein fractions and their members, fall under the broader domain of blood substitutes and perfusion solutions. This hierarchical organization clarifies how various blood‑derived and synthetic products are related, aiding clinicians in selecting appropriate substitutes and understanding their place within the overall spectrum of blood‑related therapeutics.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 1768
retrieved_docs.content: Acute posthemorrhagic anemia is a specific form of فقر الدم (anemia) that arises rapidly after substantial blood loss, leading to a sudden decrease in circulating red blood cells and hemoglobin levels. As a subtype of anemia, it shares the fundamental characteristic of reduced oxygen‑carrying capacity of the blood, but its acute onset distinguishes it from chronic or nutritional anemias. Prompt recognition and treatment of acute posthemorrhagic anemia are essential in patient care, as rapid restoration of intravascular volume and red cell mass—through fluid resuscitation, blood transfusion, and control of the bleeding source—are required to prevent tissue hypoxia, organ dysfunction, and hemodynamic instability. Understanding that acute posthemorrhagic anemia falls under the broader category of فقر الدم helps clinicians classify the condition, anticipate its pathophysiology, and apply appropriate therapeutic strategies within the overall framework of anemia management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1723
retrieved_docs.content: BLOOD SUBSTITUTES AND PERFUSION SOLUTIONS represent a broad therapeutic category that encompasses both i.v. solution additives and irrigating solutions, blood substitutes and perfusion agents. Within this hierarchy, i.v. solution additives are classified as a subset of BLOOD SUBSTITUTES AND PERFUSION SOLUTIONS, indicating that intravenous additives used for fluid replacement, drug delivery, or nutritional support belong to the larger group of blood substitutes and perfusion products. Further refining this classification, Other i.v. solution additives in ATC are identified as a specific subgroup of i.v. solution additives, highlighting a distinct set of intravenous additives catalogued in the Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) system. Additionally, irrigating solutions, blood substitutes and perfusion are themselves a type of BLOOD SUBSTITUTES AND PERFUSION SOLUTIONS, underscoring that solutions used for irrigation, as well as blood‑substituting and perfusion agents, share the same overarching category. This integrated taxonomy aids clinicians and pharmacologists in organizing fluid therapy options, selecting appropriate intravenous additives, and understanding the relationships among various blood substitute and perfusion products for optimal patient care.
